title: Adam as a she
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Jul 20, 2011 10:27 am CJD wrote: Anyway, I see this a lot. Why do we call Adam a female? Is she referenced as female in one of the resources?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 20, 2011 12:30 pm Azathoth wrote: Kaworu refers to Adam as his "mother being". ymmv on whether that indicates anything meaningful about Adam, but both Seeds of Life do appear to have characteristics that humans would associate with femininity.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jul 20, 2011 3:28 pm Also, Adam means "Man" (Or the sorts) in Jewish.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Jul 20, 2011 3:34 pm Correct me if I'm wrong, but I think Adam is a pun on the words for man and dirt.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jul 20, 2011 4:18 pm It means, "The Corporeal One".

[]:             Quote Post by Braulio » Thu Jul 21, 2011 5:25 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Correct me if I'm wrong, but I think Adam is a pun on the words for man and dirt.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2011 6:00 pm Braulio wrote: Truly? Either I have to see NGE again

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 21, 2011 6:34 pm Reichu wrote: ...Which is kind of weird considering Adam is referred to as a "he" in Evangelion's project proposal and even by Anno in an interview (though I don't know if the interview took place before or after the scripting of episode 24). As if to reinforce the confusion, the Angel statue that was eventually added as Kaworu's avatar has extremely prominent breasts.

[]:             Quote Post by Braulio » Thu Jul 21, 2011 6:45 pm CJD wrote: Personally, I don't think "Mother Being" specifically requires "Female". We refer to ships in the feminine, and yet they are just objects, they possess no gender. The "Mother Being", in this case, is based on the fact that all the angels came from Adam, He/She birthed them. That's the way I understand it, sure Kaworu could have said "Father Being", but, like you said, that doesn't jam with the whole "Mother" theme in Eva, nor is it as powerful as the phrase "Mother Being", it doesn't evoke the same emotions as "mother".

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 21, 2011 7:50 pm Braulio wrote: Yeah, since the image of father is "opresion" it wouldn't fit completly well. Still "Personally, I don't think "Mother Being" specifically requires "Female"." WTF? I haven't see a man having a child, except for that transexual. It would also fit in the image of a complete human, also called androgynous, by Platon in The Sympossium , since Adam is the original form of the Angels in NGE and of humanity in the Judeo-Christian-Muslim mythology

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2011 8:01 pm CJD wrote: Personally, I don't think "Mother Being" specifically requires "Female".

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 21, 2011 8:22 pm Reichu wrote: Not so relevant to the Adam thing, since the Japanese don't share the Freudian "ships are the only women those poor bastards stuck at sea for months have" practice.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 21, 2011 8:32 pm Reichu wrote: According to the show itself, Adam is, in fact, human. (C.f. episode 08.) Granted, this is a definition of "human" more expansive than "Lilin", but nonetheless...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2011 8:39 pm CJD wrote: I'm confused by the "C.F.", are you saying in Episode 8 he/she's referred to as human? I'm going to have to rewatch it then for the context.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 21, 2011 8:47 pm Reichu wrote: It was supposed to mean "see:", but it looks like I did it wrong. My bad.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 21, 2011 8:54 pm Bagheera wrote: No, you did it right. That's how it's supposed to be used.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2011 8:56 pm Bagheera wrote: I think everything with a FAR-derived soul is considered human.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 21, 2011 9:01 pm Reichu wrote: That's assuming that Lilith didn't keep the FAR souls on ice until an appropriate host species had evolved. (Surely, the FAR were not the only souled species on their world...) And even if she hadn't, it's possible that "humanity" has both a physical and a spiritual component. Hence why, for example, the FAR's emissaries are giant humans, all of Adam's children are intrinsically human, and the Lilin have achieved a suspiciously high degree of evolutionary convergence to the Original Model. In other words: not just any vessel will do. If one could salvage a human soul into a guinea pig, the sum result would not be a human. Regardless of whether or not the FAR believed -- as some of their descendants do -- that the humanoid form is the Ultimate Form, it might be considered a necessary component of preserving what they held dearest.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2011 9:14 pm Bagheera wrote: Given the myriad forms of the Angels I find that dubious. In fact, I'm hard-pressed to see how Iruel or Bardiel has more in common with Lilin than Lilin do with rodents. If we were just talking about SoLs sapience would work, but is Gaghiel sapient? Shamshel? Ramiel? It's not at all clear.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 21, 2011 9:32 pm Reichu wrote: Naturally it isn't. The Angels are swathed in mystery, so we have to take a lot of things "at the show's word". They embryonically start out as humans, and are still "human" and "people" even after turning into totally bizarre things. I think the idea is that Angels are intrinsically the same as any of us... only their mental self-image is effectively the same as their external self-image. "I am what I imagine myself to be", literally. Their very fundamentally different relationship between self and body (and self and environment, since they ARE their own technology), along with most of their basic needs being attended to from the start, sets the foundations for a very different human existence... one so foreign to us that the Angels' humanity is almost completely obscured from Lilin's POV.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 20, 2011 12:30 pm Azathoth wrote: Kaworu refers to Adam as his "mother being". ymmv on whether that indicates anything meaningful about Adam,

Article: Adam as a she
 In fact, I'm hard-pressed to see how Iruel or Bardiel has more in common with Lilin than Lilin do with rodents. If we were just talking about SoLs sapience would work, but is Gaghiel sapient? Shamshel? Ramiel? It's not at all clear.

They embryonically start out as humans, and are still "human" and "people" even after turning into totally bizarre things. I think the idea is that Angels are intrinsically the same as any of us... only their mental self-image is effectively the same as their external self-image. "I am what I imagine myself to be", literally. Their very fundamentally different relationship between self and body (and self and environment, since they ARE their own technology), along with most of their basic needs being attended to from the start, sets the foundations for a very different human existence... one so foreign to us that the Angels' humanity is almost completely obscured from Lilin's POV.
